$comment: #297: Reference-task catalog for the eval harness. Each entry is one task that gets run against each preset (or a subset). Add new tasks by appending to the array; the harness picks them up on next run. Keep tasks small so the full sweep finishes in a few hours.

tasks.title: Audit README claims for accuracy
tasks.directive: Read the README. For each feature claimed, verify the code actually implements it. List discrepancies (claims with no implementation; implementation with no docs).
tasks.$rationale: Pure analysis task. Tests whether each preset can produce a coherent audit list. Blackboard included because its planner can extract auditable claims into todos.

tasks.title: Resolve TODO/FIXME comments
tasks.directive: Find every TODO, FIXME, and XXX comment in src/. For each, either resolve it (commit a fix) or convert it to an issue-tracker reference. Be conservative — only fix when the resolution is clear from context.
tasks.$rationale: Code-modification task. Blackboard-only because other presets are discussion-only. Tight scope (per-comment small commits) suits the worker pipeline.

tasks.title: Choose: stay with Express or migrate to Fastify?
tasks.directive: Evaluate whether this project should keep using Express or migrate to Fastify. Consider performance, ecosystem maturity, migration cost, and team familiarity. Produce a clear recommendation with rationale.
tasks.proposition: This project should migrate from Express to Fastify.
tasks.$rationale: Decision task with two clear sides. Council parallel-drafts pros/cons; debate-judge formal PRO/CON; role-diff different angles. Tests preset suitability for architectural decisions.

tasks.title: Build a coverage map of which modules are well-tested vs not
tasks.directive: Walk the repo. For each source file, identify whether it has corresponding tests, what fraction of its API surface is covered, and where the test gaps are. Produce a coverage table.
tasks.$rationale: Repo-walk task. Stigmergy's pheromone-trail design fits naturally; map-reduce's per-file slicing also works; round-robin baseline.

tasks.title: Fix the countDown off-by-one (fixture mode)
tasks.directive: src/countdown.js has a loop that exits one iteration too early — countDown(3) returns [3, 2] instead of [3, 2, 1]. Change the loop terminator so the returned array includes the final 1. Make the smallest possible edit; do not refactor.
tasks.$rationale: First fixture-mode catalog entry — exercises end-to-end fixture staging + verify. Run: node eval/run-eval.mjs --fixture-dir=eval/fixtures --only=fixture-fix-off-by-one --presets=baseline. Other fixtures (add-null-guard, extract-pure-helper, fix-failing-test, audit-console-logs, categorize-deps, multistep-add-script, multistep-config-then-test, rename-symbol, add-readme-section) follow the same shape — add their catalog entries when ready to sweep.

$presetOverrides.$comment: Per-preset minimum-agent overrides. Catalog tasks can specify smaller agentCount; the harness clamps up to preset.min.
